2605.14892.title: Beyond Individual Intelligence: Surveying Collaboration, Failure Attribution, and Self-Evolution in LLM-based Multi-Agent Systems
2605.14892.paper_summary: LLM-based autonomous agents have demonstrated strong capabilities in reasoning, planning, and tool use, yet remain limited when tasks require sustained coordination across roles, tools, and environments. Multi-agent systems address this through structured collaboration among specialized agents, but tighter coordination also amplifies a less explored risk: errors can propagate across agents and interaction rounds, producing failures that are difficult to diagnose and rarely translate into structural self-improvement. Existing surveys cover individual agent capabilities, multi-agent collaboration, or agent self-evolution separately, leaving the causal dependencies among them unexamined. This survey provides a unified review organized around four causally linked stages, which we term the LIFE progression: Lay the capability foundation, Integrate agents through collaboration, Find faults through attribution, and Evolve through autonomous self-improvement. For each stage, we provide systematic taxonomies and formally characterize the dependencies between adjacent stages, revealing how each stage both depends on and constrains the next. Beyond synthesizing existing work, we identify open challenges at stage boundaries and propose a cross-stage research agenda for closed-loop multi-agent systems capable of continuously diagnosing failures, reorganizing structures, and refining agent behaviors, extending current coordination frameworks toward more self-organizing forms of collective intelligence. By bridging these previously fragmented research threads, this survey aims to offer both a systematic reference and a conceptual roadmap toward autonomous, self-improving multi-agent intelligence.
2605.14892.paper_summary_zh: LLM 基礎的自主代理已展現出在推理、規劃和工具使用方面的強大能力，但在任務需要跨角色、工具和環境的持續協調時仍然受到限制。多代理系統通過專門代理之間的結構化協作來解決這一問題，但更緊密的協調也放大了一個較少探索的風險：錯誤可能在代理和互動回合之間傳播，產生難以診斷的失敗，並且很少轉化為結構性的自我改進。現有的調查分別涵蓋了單一代理能力、多代理協作或代理自我演化，卻未檢視它們之間的因果依賴關係。本調查提供了一個統一的回顧，圍繞四個因果相連的階段進行組織，我們稱之為 LIFE 進程：奠定能力基礎、通過協作整合代理、通過歸因尋找故障，以及通過自主自我改進進化。對於每個階段，我們提供系統的分類法並正式描述相鄰階段之間的依賴關係，揭示每個階段如何依賴於並約束下一階段。除了綜合現有的研究外，我們還在階段邊界識別出開放挑戰，並提出了一個跨階段的研究議程，針對能夠持續診斷故障、重組結構和細化代理行為的閉環多代理系統，將當前的協調框架擴展到更具自組織形式的集體智慧。通過橋接這些先前分散的研究線索，本調查旨在提供一個系統的參考和一個概念性路線圖，朝向自主、自我改進的多代理智慧。
2605.14892.author: Shihao Qi et.al.
2605.14892.authors: Shihao Qi, Jie Ma, Rui Xing, Wei Guo, Xiao Huang, Zhitao Gao, Jianhao Deng, Jun Liu, Lingling Zhang, Bifan Wei, Boqian Yang, Pinghui Wang, Jianwen Sun, Jing Tao, Yaqiang Wu, Hui Liu, Yu Yao, Tongliang Liu
